TORNADO REPORTED IN EARLY

THE TOWN OF EARLY EXPERIENCED WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE ON THE EAST SIDE OF TOWN. SEVERAL TREES WERE TOPPLED AND MANY LIMBS WERE BROKEN OFF. A FEW BUSINESSES AND RESIDENCES LOST THEIR ROOFS. MOST OF THE DEBRIS WAS ORIENTED FROM NORTHEAST TO SOUTHWEST WHICH IS CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE. THESE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 80 TO 85 MPH.

THE TOWN OF BROWNWOOD EXPERIENCED WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE ACROSS THE EAST CENTRAL PART OF TOWN. WALMART... KROGERS...DOLLAR GENERAL STORE...AND A FEW OTHER BUSINESSES...EXPERIENCED ROOF DAMAGE. THE ROOFS WERE PEELED IN A NORTHEAST TO SOUTHWEST DIRECTION. SOME LARGE OAK TREES WERE ALSO BLOWN OVER. THIS IS ALSO CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 80 TO 85 MPH. WE ARE THANKFUL TO THE CITY OF BROWNWOOD EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND THE FIRE DEPARTMENT FOR THEIR ASSISTANCE.
FLOODING IN BROWNWOOD. SHELTERS ARE OPEN FOR PERSONS IN FLOODED HOMES. ROADWAYS ARE FLOODED...TRAVEL IS DISCOURAGED.
PART OF ROOF BLOWN OFF OF RESIDENCE.

TENNIS BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED 13 MILES NORTH OF MASON AT 925 PM.
A FEW ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF AND TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EVIDENCE OF AN F1 TRACK LOCATED ABOUT 9 MILES SOUTHEAST OF MASON. THE TORNADO WAS 200 YARDS WIDE. IT DAMAGED OR UPROOTED NUMEROUS LARGE OAK TREES. THE TORNADO PUSHED A MANUFACTURED HOME OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION...DESTROYED THE PORCH AND AN OUTBUILDING...AND DAMAGED A ROOF OF ANOTHER NEARBY HOME. THE TORNADO MOVED FROM SOUTHEAST TO NORTHWEST. SPECIAL THANKS TO LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIALS AND JUDGE FROM MASON COUNTY...AND THOSE AFFECTED BY THE TORNADO FOR THEIR HELP.
FLASH FLOODING OCCURRING AT SEVERAL LOCATIONS IN BRADY. BARRICADES WERE BEING SET UP BY LAW ENFORCEMENT.
ROOF BLOWN OFF HOUSE IN SOUTHWESTERN BRADY.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL 13 MILES NORTHWEST OF MASON AT 1210 AM.
HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL ON EAST SIDE OF ROSCOE.

A SURVEY TEAM FOUND A 1/4 MILE WIDE EF2 TORNADO THAT TORE THE ROOF OFF OF 4 HOMES AND DESTROYED SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS...IRRIGATION SPRINKLERS...AND MANY POWER POLES. THE TORNADO TRACKED FROM 3 MILES WNW OF RULE TO 1/2 MILE SOUTHEAST OF RULE BEFORE IT DISSIPATED. A SPECIAL THANKS TO THE HASKELL COUNTY SHERRIFS OFFICE AND THE WEST TEXAS MESONET FOR THEIR ASSISTANCE. THE TORNADO ENDED AT 647 PM CDT.
WIND GUSTS FROM 60 TO 70 MPH REPORTED IN RULE AT 645 PM.
NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN RULE AT 645 PM.
WEST TEXAS MESONET IN HASKELL MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 68 MPH AT 710 PM BEFORE LOSING COMMUNICATIONS.
A TELEPHONE POLE WAS BLOWN DOWN.
GUST FRONT WELL AHEAD OF THE STORM.
POWER LINES KNOCKED DOWN...GARBAGE DUMPSTERS TURNED OVER.
STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS FROM 65 TO 75 MPH DAMAGED A SHED USED TO HOUSE A RECREATIONAL VEHICLE...AND LARGE TREE LIMBS OF 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
OFF DUTY POLICE OFFICER REPORTED 75 TO 80 MPH WINDS IN ROSCOE.
THE WEST TEXAS MESONET STATION LOCATED A MILE NORTHWEST OF HASKELL MEASURED THE STRONG THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.
A TRAINED STORM SPOTTER REPORTED WIND DAMAGE FROM THUNDERSTORM. IT DAMAGED A TIN BUILDING...A COUPLE OF ROOFS IN TOWN.
WIND GUSTS OF 60 MPH AND 56 MPH WERE REPORTED RESPECTIVELY FROM THE KTAB AND KTXS NEWS STATIONS.
MEASURED BY REPORTING STATION AT DYESS AFB.
SHERIFF DEPUTY REPORTED THAT A ROOF WAS TAKEN OFF OF A STORAGE BUILDING AND A UTILITY POLE WAS SNAPPED IN HALF ON THE EAST SIDE OF ELDORADO. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.
SEVERAL POWERLINES AND TREES ARE DOWN IN WINTERS.
